req.title: Export employee directory — variant 5
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path export by hr-admin with 2FA and correct PII redaction (EP), (2) access denial for non-hr-admin role (NEG), (3) rate-limit boundary at the 429 threshold (BVA), (4) decision-table covering the four combinations of opt-out flag × PII visibility (DT), and (5) state-transition from unauthenticated → 2FA-pending → 2FA-confirmed → export-granted (STC). Tests are self-contained via fixtures and helpers; each touches a single concern.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out_employees
preconditions: At least one employee has opted out of public directory
At least one employee has NOT opted out
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
Complete 2FA confirmation to receive export-scoped token
expected: Rows for opted-out employees have PII fields replaced with REDACTED
Rows for non-opted-out employees contain real PII values
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv
import io

def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out_employees(
    hr_admin_2fa_token,
    opted_out_employee_id,
    opted_in_employee_id,
    api_client,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_2fa_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in resp.headers["Content-Type"]

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in reader}

    opted_out_row = rows[opted_out_employee_id]
    assert opted_out_row["email"] == "REDACTED"
    assert opted_out_row["phone"] == "REDACTED"

    opted_in_row = rows[opted_in_employee_id]
    assert opted_in_row["email"] != "REDACTED"
    assert opted_in_row["phone"] != "REDACTED"

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_export_returns_403
preconditions: Valid bearer token for that role exists
actions: Authenticate as regular employee and obtain bearer token
expected: Response body contains error code or message indicating insufficient privileges
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_hr_admin_export_returns_403(
    employee_token,
    api_client,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_export_rate_limit_returns_429_at_threshold
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin with valid 2FA token
Rate-limit window is fresh (no prior requests counted)
Known rate-limit cap N is available via config
actions: Send exactly N requests — expect all to succeed with 200
Send the (N+1)-th request in the same window
expected: Response headers include Retry-After or X-RateLimit-Reset
tests.executable:
import pytest
from config import EXPORT_RATE_LIMIT_CAP

def test_export_rate_limit_returns_429_at_threshold(
    hr_admin_2fa_token,
    api_client,
    reset_rate_limit_for_hr_admin,
):
    reset_rate_limit_for_hr_admin()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_2fa_token}"}
    url = "/api/employees/export.csv"

    for i in range(EXPORT_RATE_LIMIT_CAP):
        r = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Request {i+1} unexpectedly failed"

    over_limit = api_client.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    assert (
        "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers
        or "X-RateLimit-Reset" in over_limit.headers
    )

tests.name: test_export_pii_visibility_decision_table_all_combinations
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin with valid 2FA token
Four seed employees exist covering all opt-out × PII-populated combinations
actions: Parse CSV and evaluate each row against expected redaction rule
expected: opted_out=true, pii_present=true  → PII columns show REDACTED
opted_out=true, pii_present=false → PII columns show REDACTED (or empty)
opted_out=false, pii_present=true → PII columns show real values
opted_out=false, pii_present=false → PII columns are empty string, not REDACTED
trace: DT: all four opt-out × pii-present combinations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv
import io

def test_export_pii_visibility_decision_table_all_combinations(
    hr_admin_2fa_token,
    api_client,
    dt_seed_employees,
):
    # dt_seed_employees returns dict keyed by (opted_out, pii_present) -> employee_id
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_2fa_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))}

    cases = [
        (True,  True,  "REDACTED"),
        (True,  False, "REDACTED"),
        (False, True,  None),
        (False, False, ""),
    ]
    for opted_out, pii_present, expected_email in cases:
        eid = dt_seed_employees[(opted_out, pii_present)]
        row = rows[eid]
        if expected_email is None:
            assert row["email"] not in ("", "REDACTED")
        else:
            assert row["email"] == expected_email

tests.name: test_export_requires_2fa_state_transition
preconditions: Caller has a standard bearer token (1FA only, 2FA NOT yet completed)
actions: Complete 2FA to upgrade to export-scoped token
expected: First attempt returns HTTP 403 or 401 with error indicating 2FA required
Second attempt (post-2FA) returns HTTP 200 with CSV body
trace: R-WEB-086: additional variant semantics — 2FA confirmation required
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_export_requires_2fa_state_transition(
    hr_admin_1fa_token,
    complete_2fa_for_hr_admin,
    api_client,
):
    url = "/api/employees/export.csv"

    # State: 2FA-pending — should be blocked
    pre_2fa = api_client.get(
        url,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_1fa_token}"},
    )
    assert pre_2fa.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = pre_2fa.json()
    assert any(
        kw in str(body).lower()
        for kw in ("2fa", "two-factor", "mfa", "otp")
    )

    # State: 2FA-confirmed — should succeed
    export_token = complete_2fa_for_hr_admin(hr_admin_1fa_token)
    post_2fa = api_client.get(
        url,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {export_token}"},
    )
    assert post_2fa.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in post_2fa.headers["Content-Type"]
